key_insight: Replayed-Prefix On-Policy Distillation (ReOPD) turns costly multi-turn online distillation into an offline process by reusing teacher trajectories as prefix replay while carefully balancing student relevance against teacher reliability.
engineering_relevance: This makes agent distillation for tool-using reasoning models far cheaper and more scalable, with zero tool calls during student training and much faster training steps.
method: Reuses pre-collected teacher trajectories as replayed prefixes instead of fresh environment rollouts.
Lets the student act at selected steps while the teacher gives dense per-step supervision on the prefix.
Uses a step-decaying sampling schedule to favor early prefixes where teacher targets are more reliable.
evidence_signal: Across mathematical reasoning tasks with Python and search environments, ReOPD matches or improves OPD-level accuracy and is at least 4× faster per training step.
watch_item: The prefix schedule must be tuned carefully, because pushing too far toward student-on-policy histories can trigger a reliability mismatch where teacher targets become less trustworthy.
paper_title: Multi-Turn On-Policy Distillation with Prefix Replay
